item.title: World Bank Targets AI-Resilient Sectors to Boost Job Creation
item.description: The World Bank is adjusting its playbook to account for the potential impact of artificial intelligence on workers, as it seeks to boost job creation in the poorest parts of the world.

item.title: Gold-Mining Stocks Set to Erase 2026 Gains as Rate-Cut Bets Fade
item.description: Global gold-mining stocks tumbled, and are now in the red for this year, as traders ratcheted back expectations for interest-rate cuts with oil prices surging amid the Iran war.

item.title: Goldman Sachs AM Seeks to Raise $10 Billion Private Credit Fund
item.description: Goldman Sachs Asset Management has begun preliminary talks with investors to raise at least $10 billion for a global direct lending fund, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: DoorDash’s New Paid Tasks Turn Couriers Into AI and Robot Trainers
item.description: DoorDash Inc. is paying delivery couriers in some markets to submit video clips and complete other digital tasks to help improve artificial intelligence and robotics models, following competitors that have found creative new uses for gig workers in the AI boom.

item.title: AI Coding Startup Cursor Plans New Model to Rival Anthropic, OpenAI
item.description: Cursor, a leading artificial intelligence startup for coding, is set to release a more efficient AI model for software development in a bid to keep pace with larger firms like Anthropic PBC and OpenAI.

item.title: Startup Oasis Security Raises $120 Million From Craft, Sequoia
item.description: Oasis Security, a cybersecurity startup that helps companies manage access to their systems from non-human accounts such as artificial intelligence agents, has raised $120 million from investors including Sequoia Capital and Accel.

item.title: Colombia Oil Workers Threaten Strike Unless Ecopetrol CEO Quits
item.description: Colombia’s biggest oil workers union is threatening to strike unless Ecopetrol SA’s board ousts Chief Executive Officer Ricardo Roa over charges of alleged influence peddling.

item.title: Singer’s Elliott Backs Credit Veteran Maji’s New CLO Hedge Fund
item.description: Billionaire Paul Singer’s Elliott Investment Management is backing credit specialist Debdeep Maji’s new hedge fund, the latest move in a wave of multistrategy firms tapping outside talent to help manage their billions.

item.title: Kazakh Construction Tycoon Mutalip Buys Gold Miner Altynalmas
item.description: One of Kazakhstan’s most prominent construction tycoons agreed to buy gold producer JSC AK Altynalmas and its units as he continues to expand his business interests.

item.title: Billionaire Bolloré Faces French Corruption Trial Over Togo Port
item.description: Billionaire Vincent Bolloré was ordered to face trial in December over allegations including bribery to win a port contract in Togo a decade and a half ago.

item.title: Bonds Tumble Worldwide as Iran War Stokes Bets on Rate Hikes
item.description: Bonds tumbled worldwide as investors rushed to bet on higher interest rates after key central banks signaled fresh concern the surge in oil prices will deliver an inflation shock.

item.title: JPMorgan, Goldman Offer Hedge Funds Way to Short Private Credit
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. and JPMorgan Chase & Co. are among investment banks offering hedge fund clients ways to bet against the $1.8 trillion private credit market, people with knowledge of the matter said.

item.title: Societe Generale Weighs SRT Deal Linked to Data Center Lending
item.description: Societe Generale SA is mulling a significant risk transfer of some of its exposure to data centers as part of plans to manage the amount of credit the lender has advanced to the rapidly growing asset class.

item.title: Air Baltic’s Bonds Tumble as Mideast War Drives Up Fuel Costs
item.description: Air Baltic Corp AS’s bonds have plunged as a surge in the aviation fuel price raised concerns over how the struggling Latvian airline will shoulder higher costs.

item.title: BNP Paribas Predicts Fed Next Month Will Flag Possible Rate Hike
item.description: Interest rate strategists at BNP Paribas expect the Federal Reserve will raise the possibility of hiking rates at its next meeting in April if energy prices remain high and the US unemployment rate stays stable.

item.title: Indonesia to Raise Coal Output as Iran War Triggers Price Spike
item.description: Indonesia will allow miners to boost coal production after prices surged following disruptions to global energy markets caused by the war in Iran.

item.title: US New-Home Sales Fell to the Lowest Since 2022 in January
item.description: Sales of new US homes fell by more than expected in January to the lowest since 2022, reflecting a pullback in buyer demand across the country.

item.title: Alliança Saúde Seeks Temporary Court Protection to Rework Debt
item.description: Alliança Saúde e Participações SA said it is seeking temporary court protection in Brazil and starting mediation talks with creditors as it struggles to meet upcoming debt payments.

item.title: Global Trade to Slow Amid Opposing Forces of Energy Surge and AI
item.description: Global merchandise trade will decelerate less sharply this year than the World Trade Organization predicted six months ago, but faces a deeper slowdown if war in the Middle East keeps energy prices high for a sustained period.

item.title: Trump’s New Data Chief Will Face Intense Scrutiny and a Shrunken Workforce
item.description: The Bureau of Labor Statistics has shrunk since Brett Matsumoto left it a year ago – but the job he’s poised to take on is bigger than ever.

item.title: Iran Strike to Cut 17% of Qatari LNG for 3-5 Years: Reuters
item.description: Iran’s latest attack on Qatar damaged facilities that produce about 17% of its liquefied natural gas export capacity, Reuters reported, citing QatarEnergy’s chief executive officer.

item.title: US Regulators Unveil Plans to Ease Capital Rules for Big Banks
item.description: Wall Street lending giants would get relaxed capital requirements under proposals unveiled by the Federal Reserve on Thursday, in a move that could potentially unleash billions of dollars for lending, share buybacks and dividends.

item.title: Czechs Hold Rates With Policy Outlook Clouded by Energy Shock
item.description: Czech policymakers kept interest rates on hold, with investors awaiting signals about the outlook for monetary policy and market prices signaling bets on rapid rate hikes.

item.title: MLB Inks Prediction Market Agreements With Polymarket, CFTC
item.description: Major League Baseball has entered new agreements with Polymarket and the federal regulator that oversees prediction markets, formalizing the sports league’s involvement in the fast-growing industry.

item.title: Cosco Shipping Warns of Uncertainties as Iran War Escalates
item.description: China’s largest container liner Cosco Shipping Holdings Co. warned of increasing uncertainties in the year ahead as a deepening Middle East conflict continues to reshape global trade.

item.title: ECB Holds Rates Steady as Officials Measure Cost of Iran War
item.description: The European Central Bank kept interest rates unchanged for a sixth meeting as it warned that the war in Iran could shift its expectations for inflation and the economy.

item.title: BofA’s Hartnett Says Consumer Stocks Best Bet as S&P 500 Wavers
item.description: Consumer stocks are the best buying opportunity in the current environment, according to Bank of America Corp.’s Michael Hartnett, even as a surge in benchmark oil prices to near multi-year highs threatens to fan inflation and dent economic growth.

item.title: OpenAI to Acquire Startup Astral, Expanding Push Into Coding
item.description: OpenAI plans to acquire Astral, a startup that makes Python tools for developers, marking the ChatGPT maker’s latest investment in the fast-growing market for coding and developer services.

item.title: Aluminum Falls More Than 8% on LME, Biggest Drop Since 2018
item.description: Aluminum plunged by the most since 2018 on the London Metal Exchange as growing worries about the global economic impact of the war in Iran fueled broad losses across industrial metals markets.

item.title: Echostar Restructuring Paves Way to Revive Possible DirecTV Deal
item.description: Echostar Corp. has reached a restructuring agreement with a majority of creditors in its Dish DBS unit, which would potentially pave the way to revive an M&A deal with DirecTV.
